para.verbatim: creates a vector with \\p size elements with default value
creates a vector with \\p size elements with the given value
moves data from the given std::vector<T>
this accessor automatically adjusts the size of the vector

memberdef.argsstring: (self, GraphVertId pos, int len, FaceBitSet val)
memberdef.name: autoResizeSet

para.verbatim: sets elements [pos, pos+len) to the given value, adjusting the size of the vector to include new elements; the elements in between old size and \\p pos are also set to \\p val (for faster implementation)
sets the element #i to the given value, adjusting the size of the vector to include new element
returns the identifier of the back() element
returns the identifier of the first element
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap
doubles reserved memory until resize(newSize) can be done without reallocation
doubles reserved memory until resize(newSize, value) can be done without reallocation
The user can directly manipulate the vector, they can't break anything anyway.

Generated from: MR::Vector<MR::FaceBitSet, MR::GraphVertId>
\\brief std::vector<T>-like container that requires specific indexing type, \\tparam T type of stored elements \\tparam I type of index (shall be convertible to size_t)
